changed.from: Fix an observed-data law P in P_atom(M). Within the local extension class H_*(P,M), the set of selected local net escalation values compatible with P is exactly [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)]; equivalently, {mu(tau_*(P)) : mu in H_*(P,M)} = [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)]. Moreover each point of [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] is attained by some mu in H_*(P,M).
changed.to: Fix an observed-data law P in P_atom(M) and suppose H_*(P,M) is nonempty. Within the local extension class H_*(P,M), the set of selected local net escalation values compatible with P is exactly [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)]; equivalently, {mu(tau_*(P)) : mu in H_*(P,M)} = [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)]. Moreover each point of [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] is attained by some mu in H_*(P,M).
changed.reason: As stated, P in P_atom(M) does not by itself guarantee that the range-respecting extension class H_*(P,M) is nonempty. Without nonemptiness, the endpoint functionals and the sharp-set claim are not mathematically well posed.

changed.from: Under the assumptions of thm:selected-sharp-set, M -> L_*(P,M) is weakly decreasing and M -> U_*(P,M) is weakly increasing, so M_*(P) is well defined. If 0 notin [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], then M_*(P) is the sign-certification frontier: for every M < M_*(P), the interval [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] has the same strict sign as [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], while 0 in [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] for every M > M_*(P). If in addition L_*(P,M) < U_*(P,M) for some M, then there exist mu_L, mu_U in H_*(P,M) such that mu_L(s) = mu_U(s) = m_P(s) for every s in A_P intersect [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0], mu_L(tau_*(P)) = L_*(P,M), and mu_U(tau_*(P)) = U_*(P,M); hence the same observed law P, the same frontier objects K_P and Q_P, and the same selected cutoff tau_*(P) are compatible with distinct selected local boundary values. In the fixed-cutoff special case with support points arbitrarily close to that cutoff from both sides, [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] collapses to the fixed-cutoff point value.
changed.to: Under the narrowed assumptions of thm:selected-sharp-set for every curvature budget and, in particular, assuming H_*(P,0) is nonempty, M -> L_*(P,M) is weakly decreasing and M -> U_*(P,M) is weakly increasing, so M_*(P) is well defined. If 0 notin [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], then M_*(P) is the sign-certification frontier: for every M < M_*(P), the interval [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] has the same strict sign as [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], while 0 in [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] for every M > M_*(P). If in addition L_*(P,M) < U_*(P,M) for some M, then there exist mu_L, mu_U in H_*(P,M) such that mu_L(s) = mu_U(s) = m_P(s) for every s in A_P intersect [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0], mu_L(tau_*(P)) = L_*(P,M), and mu_U(tau_*(P)) = U_*(P,M); hence the same observed law P, the same frontier objects K_P and Q_P, and the same selected cutoff tau_*(P) are compatible with distinct selected local boundary values. In the fixed-cutoff special case with support points arbitrarily close to that cutoff from both sides, [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] collapses to the fixed-cutoff point value.
changed.reason: The proposition varies the curvature budget all the way from 0 upward and explicitly references [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)]. That requires nonemptiness of H_*(P,0); otherwise the zero-curvature interval and the frontier M_*(P) are not defined on the stated domain.

changed.from: H_*(P,M) = {mu in C^2([tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0]) : mu(s) = m_P(s) for each s in A_P intersect [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0], sup_{c in [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0]} |mu''(c)| <= M, and mu(c) in [-2,1] for every c in [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0]}
changed.to: H_*(P,M) = {mu in W^{2,\infty}([tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0]) : mu(s) = m_P(s) for each s in A_P intersect [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0], ess sup_{c in [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0]} |mu''(c)| <= M, and mu(c) in [-2,1] for every c in [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0]}
changed.reason: The theorem and proposition need endpoint attainment in the curvature-budget class. With strict C^2, the feasible set need not be compact enough for extrema of the point-evaluation functional to be attained, whereas honest-RD extremizers naturally live in the closed bounded-curvature class with Lipschitz derivative. Replacing C^2 by W^{2,\infty} (equivalently C^{1,1} on the compact interval) preserves the intended second-derivative budget while making the admissible class closed under the compactness argument used to attain L_*(P,M) and U_*(P,M).

note: Approved (orchestrator): all 3 faithful well-posedness refinements. thm/prop narrowings add 'H_*(P,M) nonempty' guard (conclusions byte-identical, narrowing toward truth, not weaken-to-prove). def correction C^2->W^{2,inf}=C^{1,1} is the standard honest-RD bounded-2nd-derivative class (Armstrong-Kolesar/Kolesar-Rothe), making the class closed so endpoints L_*,U_* are attained; improves donor fidelity. No bank-down: math claim sound.

directive: OPEN-GAP GUIDANCE for conj:active-set-geometry (literature-anchored; EXTEND the proved lem:endpoint-shape-in-budget, do NOT restart). Prove the finite active-set / piecewise-affine structure in 3 steps. (1) FINITE-DIM REDUCTION via optimal-recovery perfect-spline theory: an extremizer of the point-evaluation functional mu|->mu(tau_*) over H_*(P,M) is a QUADRATIC PERFECT SPLINE -- mu''=+/-M a.e., sign-switching at <=|A_P|-1 free interior knots, C^1 across knots, plus <=2 contact knots where mu touches the range bound -2 or +1. Cite Micchelli-Rivlin 'A Survey of Optimal Recovery' (1977) and 'Optimal recovery of smooth functions' (Numer.Math.1974); Karlin, Total Positivity; Pinkus, n-Widths (perfect-spline extremals, knot count <= N-r+1 with r=2). (2) AFFINE-IN-M VALUE per combinatorial TYPE (sign pattern of mu'' + active atoms/range bounds): the knot positions + integration constant are pinned by the interpolation + C^1 + contact equations; solving them, mu(tau_*)=alpha(type)+beta(type)*M is AFFINE in M (each quadratic piece contributes (M/2)(x-xi)^2). (3) PIECEWISE-AFFINE GLOBALLY by parametric-LP/QP sensitivity (Gal-Nedoma): U_*(P,M)=sup over finitely many types of affine-in-M functions is continuous, piecewise-affine, CONCAVE in M (L_* convex, nonincreasing), breakpoints = active-set changes; M_*(P) is the first breakpoint where a curvature/range inequality switches slack->binding so 0 enters the interval (uniqueness of the extremal pair at the boundary via Donoho 1994 AoS modulus geometry). Cross-check Armstrong-Kolesar 2018 Ecta (arXiv:1511.06028, sec 2.1-2.2): their least-favorable b-function IS this contact-with-constraints extremizer and worst-case bias is affine in the curvature budget. Add this as a new lemma lem:perfect-spline-active-set feeding conj:active-set-geometry.

OPEN-GAP GUIDANCE for conj:postselect-honest-ci (literature-anchored; assemble 3 named pieces, EXTEND the recorded (i)+(ii) reduction). The first-stage calibration RATE may stay a HIGH-LEVEL assumption (consistent with the accepted scope caveat / field tier) -- do NOT try to derive it from scratch. (1) UNIFORM LOCALIZATION |hat tau - tau_*(P)|=o_P(h_n): van der Vaart-Wellner Thm 3.2.5 (M-estimator rate) + 3.2.2 (argmax CMT) under the margin/quadratic-growth ass:quadratic-selection-growth (HIGH-LEVEL: separation constant c bounded below over P). The threshold class {1(hat C<=tau)} is VC-index-1 => uniform entropy logN(eps)<~log(1/eps) => local modulus phi_n(delta)~sqrt(delta) => rate r_n~(n h_n)^{1/3} (Kim-Pollard 1990; Seo-Otsu 2018 arXiv:1610.02753). Since (n h_n)^{1/3} h_n -> inf whenever n h_n^3 -> inf, r_n^{-1}=o(h_n). You need only uniform consistency+rate, NOT the argmax limit law. (2) GENERATED REGRESSOR (cross-fitted isotonic hat C -> c_P): Mammen-Rothe-Schienle 2012 AoS (arXiv:1207.5594, Thm 1, Asm 2-3) expansion m_hat-m_tilde = -m_0'(x)*Delta_hat(x)+rem, with Hahn-Ridder 2013 Ecta Thm 5 correction d_c E[Y|c]*IF_1; ORTHOGONALIZE the local-linear score by subtracting m_0'(x)*(projection of hat C - c_P) so d_c E[psi]=0 (Neyman-orthogonal, Chernozhukov et al. 2018), and CROSS-FIT (isotonic is NOT Donsker -> cross-fitting kills the own-observation term). This makes def:generated-score-drift-handle precise. Remainder negligible under HIGH-LEVEL r_n=o(h_n), sqrt(n h_n) r_n^2 -> 0, and stochastic equicontinuity (MRS Asm 3). (3) SELECTED-CUTOFF CRITICAL VALUE cv_sel: adapt Armstrong-Kolesar 2018 ReStud bandwidth-snooping (arXiv:1412.0267, Thm 3.1, Cor 3.1, eq.(3)) -- RE-INDEX the t-stat process by cutoff tau in [hat tau - rho h, hat tau + rho h] (fixed/undersmoothed h) instead of by bandwidth; it converges to a stationary Gaussian process H_tilde(tau) with SHIFT covariance int k(u)k(u+(tau'-tau)/h)du / int k^2, and cv_sel = (1-alpha)-quantile of sup_{|tau-hat tau|<=rho h}|H_tilde(tau)|. The strong-approximation -> Gaussian-sup -> adjusted-cv machinery transplants directly; the shift kernel is smooth at 0 so a Rice/upcrossing tube bound is sharper than Darling-Erdos. Honest coverage needs bias control sup sqrt(n h)|g_P(tau)-g_P(tau_*)|/sigma <= the M-budget cushion (undersmooth n h^5 -> 0, or bias-aware with bound M). ASSEMBLY: (1) puts hat tau in the O(h)-window where (3)'s local Gaussian process is valid; (2) makes hat C behave as c_P to first order so the (3) process is unperturbed; (3)'s max-over-tau-neighborhood gives cv_sel for uniform honest coverage of V_cont(P)=g_P(tau_*(P)). If any single piece genuinely cannot close from primitives, encode it as a NAMED honest substrate-gate assumption (classical, documented) -- NOT a vacuous/laundered witness; a field-tier partial (sound interval + honest gate) is acceptable.

changed.from: Suppose the window [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0] contains finitely many score atoms, 0 notin [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], and M_*(P) in (0,infinity). Then there exists a finite partition of [0,M_*(P)] such that on each partition cell both L_*(P,M) and U_*(P,M) are affine in M, and M_*(P) is attained at the first partition boundary where the identity of an endpoint-attaining extremizer changes because at least one inequality from the curvature bound or the range restriction in H_*(P,M) becomes binding, and zero enters [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)].
changed.to: Suppose the window [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0] contains finitely many score atoms, 0 notin [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], and M_*(P) in (0,infinity). Then there exists a finite partition of [0,M_*(P)] such that on each partition cell both L_*(P,M) and U_*(P,M) are affine in M. Moreover zero first enters [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] at M=M_*(P), and M_*(P) is either (i) a partition boundary where the identity of an endpoint-attaining extremizer changes because a curvature or range inequality in H_*(P,M) switches binding status, or (ii) the unique zero of the active affine endpoint on a partition cell whose endpoint-attaining extremizer does not change across that cell.
changed.reason: The finite-piece affine geometry is provable from the perfect-spline reduction, but the final sentence of the current claim is false as stated. A two-atom witness shows the problem. Take I=[0,1], tau_*(P)=1/2, A_P intersect I={0,1}, and m_P(0)=m_P(1)=1. Then for 0<=M<=8 the lower-endpoint extremizer is the single quadratic perfect spline mu_M(c)=1+(M/2)(c^2-c), so L_*(P,M)=mu_M(1/2)=1-M/8 and U_*(P,M)=1. Hence M_*(P)=8, zero first enters exactly when the same affine branch L_*(P,M)=1-M/8 crosses zero, and there is no change in the identity of the endpoint-attaining extremizer at M_*(P); the curvature constraint is already binding for every M>0 and the range restriction is slack at M=8. Thus the statement is too strong only in its characterization of how the frontier must be attained, not in the piecewise-affine decomposition itself.

changed.from: Construct hat G_n by orthogonalizing the selected local-linear score with respect to local perturbations of the calibration map c_P, estimating the resulting first-order influence contribution by cross-fitting over the selected score window, and then taking a data-only envelope for the residual score-discretization remainder.
changed.to: Construct hat G_n as a data-only envelope for the entire selected-score feasibility gap: combine (a) an orthogonalized, cross-fitted first-order correction for replacing the population score C with the generated score hat C in the local-linear score, with (b) a selected-threshold localization envelope for the additional target drift created by evaluating the local-linear fit at the random selected cutoff hat tau instead of the population cutoff tau_*(P), and then add a remainder envelope for the residual second-order generated-score and score-discretization terms.
changed.reason: The proof decomposition shows that the existing construction is incomplete. Even if orthogonalization removes the first-order effect of replacing C by hat C, the estimator in def:selected-ll-estimator is centered at the random cutoff hat tau, while the target is V_cont(P)=g_P(tau_*(P)). This introduces a separate first-order drift term of size g_P(hat tau)-g_P(tau_*(P)) or, equivalently, the same shift measured inside the oracle local-linear score. That term is not a curvature-bias term and is not covered by the current wording of hat G_n, which only mentions calibration-map perturbations and score-discretization remainder. Because C_n(M) uses hat G_n as the sole non-curvature correction, the correct fix is to enlarge def:generated-score-drift-handle so that hat G_n also absorbs the selected-threshold target shift. Leaving the definition unchanged would make the downstream interval formula internally inconsistent with the proved decomposition.

note: Approved 2 of 3 (orchestrator). conj:active-set-geometry narrowing: faithful, counterexample-correct (M_* dichotomy: active-set boundary OR sign-crossing within an affine cell); keeps proved piecewise-affine decomposition. def:generated-score-drift-handle: accept — enlarges Ghat_n to a data-only envelope covering BOTH the generated-score first-order term AND the target-shift drift (g_P(tau_hat)-g_P(tau_*)); this is the constructive fix. NOT applying conj:postselect-honest-ci narrowing: its condition (ii) assumes the crux (Ghat dominates the gap) per invariant #10 / gate warning; injecting a directive to DERIVE (ii) instead, keeping (i) localization + (iii) oracle coverage as named classical gates.

directive: OPEN-GAP GUIDANCE for conj:postselect-honest-ci (round 3; the corrected def:generated-score-drift-handle now makes Ghat_n a data-only envelope covering BOTH the generated-score first-order term AND the target-shift drift from evaluating at hat tau vs tau_*(P)). DO NOT ASSUME THE CRUX: do not add a bare 'suppose Ghat_n dominates the feasibility gap' premise (the gate flagged that as assume-the-crux; invariant: the crux must be PROVEN, not assumed). Resolve as follows. (A) KEEP as EXPLICIT NAMED CLASSICAL substrate-gates (visible *_of_gate scaffolding, reported to SUBSTRATE_DEBT, dischargeable later) ONLY these two, which are about CLASSICAL objects: GATE (i) selected-threshold localization, P(|hat tau - tau_*(P)| <= rho_n h_n) -> 1 uniformly over P_cont(M) under ass:quadratic-selection-growth -- classical van der Vaart-Wellner Thm 3.2.5 (argmax/argmin rate under quadratic-growth margin) with the VC-index-1 threshold class {1(hat C<=tau)}; GATE (iii) selected-NEIGHBOURHOOD ORACLE simultaneous honest coverage with critical value cv_sel, for the ORACLE process tilde V_n(tau) (built from the POPULATION score C_i and deterministic tau) -- classical Armstrong-Kolesar 2018 ReStud bandwidth-snooping transplanted to the cutoff-indexed oracle process (strong approximation -> Gaussian sup -> adjusted cv). (B) DERIVE, do NOT assume, the envelope-validity (the old condition (ii)) as a LEMMA lem:ghat-envelope-valid: prove the corrected Ghat_n dominates the feasible-to-oracle gap w.h.p. FROM gate (i) + the smoothness class, NOT as a hypothesis. Concretely: target-shift drift |g_P(hat tau)-g_P(tau_*(P))| <= ||g_P'||_{infty,window} * |hat tau - tau_*(P)|, with ||g_P'|| bounded via the curvature class (|g_P''|<=M on the window => g_P' Lipschitz, bounded by a data-estimable local-slope envelope) and |hat tau - tau_*| <= rho_n h_n by gate (i); the generated-score term is second-order under orthogonalization + cross-fitting (Mammen-Rothe-Schienle 2012 AoS; r_n=o(h_n), sqrt(n h_n) r_n^2 -> 0); hence the def-(b) localization-envelope component of Ghat_n upper-bounds the target drift and the def-(a) component upper-bounds the generated-score term, so Ghat_n dominates the total gap w.h.p. => lem:ghat-envelope-valid PROVEN. (C) STATE the THEOREM: C_n(M) has asymptotic coverage >= 1-alpha for V_cont(P)=g_P(tau_*(P)) uniformly over P_cont(M) under ass:quadratic-selection-growth, resting on GATES (i),(iii) with (ii)=lem:ghat-envelope-valid PROVEN (not assumed). If the honest half-width ends up dominated by the target-shift term (CI shrinks slower than fixed-cutoff honest RD because rho_n h_n can exceed the SE 1/sqrt(n h) and the curvature bias M h^2), STATE that explicitly as the POST-SELECTION RATE COST -- it is a genuine finding (the price of a data-chosen cutoff), not a defect; report the resulting honest rate as a function of (M, the localization rate rho_n, h_n). Do NOT promote (ii) to a hypothesis; if some sub-step truly cannot close from primitives, leave it as a NAMED honest gate (classical, documented) -- never a vacuous/laundered witness. A field-tier result with crux (ii) DERIVED and only classical gates (i),(iii) assumed is the target.

D0.5-ESCALATION FIX (round 4 — gate hygiene via the CITED-CLASSICAL-THEOREM -> LEMMA-NODE mechanism in the solver prompt). The D0.5 referee flagged unsupported_external_reduction / gate_laundering / unsupported_width_rate because classical theorems were used inline. FIX: MATERIALIZE each relied-upon classical theorem as its OWN lemma node {id:'lem:<slug>', kind:'lemma', statement:<the exact fact used>, depends_on:[], status:'proved', proof_tex:'<author, year, named theorem, precise statement; this citation IS the proof>'} and list that lemma id in depends_on of every statement/lemma that uses it. Create these THREE classical-source lemma nodes: (A) lem:perfect-spline-extremal -- statement: an extremizer of point-evaluation mu|->mu(c0) over {mu in W^{2,infty}(I): mu(s)=m_P(s) on finite A_P, ess sup|mu''|<=M, mu in [-2,1] on I} is a quadratic perfect spline (mu''=+/-M a.e.) with at most |A_P|-1 interior sign-change knots plus at most 2 range-contact knots; proof_tex cites Micchelli-Rivlin 1977 'A Survey of Optimal Recovery' / Karlin Total Positivity (perfect-spline extremal). (B) lem:generated-score-expansion -- statement: under second-stage smoothness + stochastic equicontinuity and first-stage rate r_n=o(h_n), sqrt(n h_n) r_n^2 -> 0, the cross-fitted orthogonalized selected-score estimator satisfies m_hat-m_tilde = -m_0'(x)Delta_hat(x)+R_n with R_n second-order; proof_tex cites Mammen-Rothe-Schienle 2012 AoS Thm 1 (+ Hahn-Ridder 2013). (C) lem:oracle-snooping-coverage -- statement: for the oracle local-linear process tilde V_n(tau) (population score, deterministic tau) there is cv_sel with liminf inf_P P(sup_{|tau-tau_*|<=rho_n h_n}|tilde V_n(tau)-g_P(tau)| <= cv_sel*hat sigma_n + hat B_n(M)) >= 1-alpha, with hat sigma_n=O_P((n h_n)^{-1/2}) and hat B_n(M)=O(M h_n^2); proof_tex cites Armstrong-Kolesar 2018 ReStud 'Bandwidth Snooping' Thm 3.1/Cor 3.1 (uniform-over-continuum honest coverage, transplanted from bandwidth to cutoff index). THEN derive the NEW content with depends_on wired to A/B/C (do NOT re-cite inline): (1) lem:perfect-spline-active-set depends_on lem:perfect-spline-extremal; PROVE L_*=min_j(a_j+b_j M), U_*=max_k(alpha_k+beta_k M) (each spline pattern gives mu(tau_*) affine in M; value=min/max over finitely many patterns => piecewise-affine, elementary parametric-LP). conj:active-set-geometry follows. (2) lem:ghat-envelope-valid depends_on {lem:generated-score-expansion, lem:selected-local-slope-envelope, the localization clause}; PROVE hat G_n dominates the gap by splitting into generated-score term (lem:generated-score-expansion, second-order) + target-shift term |g_P(hat tau)-g_P(tau_*)|<=B_slope(M,h_0)*rho_n h_n (lem:selected-local-slope-envelope + localization). (3) lem:postselect-honest-ci-under-gates / conj:postselect-honest-ci depends_on lem:oracle-snooping-coverage; DERIVE the half-width rate O_P((n h_n)^{-1/2}+M h_n^2+rho_n h_n) term-by-term (SE from lem:oracle-snooping-coverage, curvature M h_n^2 from ass:curvature-budget, target-shift rho_n h_n from (2)) -- the rate now follows from declared deps. (4) thm:selected-sharp-set: drop any unused (redundant) assumption; keep only load-bearing hypotheses; conclusion unchanged. (5) lem:endpoint-shape-in-budget: wire its depends_on to the H_*(P,0)-nonemptiness assumption (ass:score-cef-extension) it already invokes. All lemma slugs lowercase-kebab [a-z0-9-]. Classical facts = A/B/C citation-lemmas (reviewer checks the cite); ALL adaptation/glue is YOUR derived step.

D0.5 REGULARITY REFINEMENTS (round 5 — the referee's 3 remaining findings are standard well-posedness/regularity conditions; ADD them as the objects' regularity, never weaken a conclusion). (1) missing_selected_cutoff_existence @ def:law-class-continuous: emit a proposed_definition_change (direction:correct) strengthening P_cont(M) so the SELECTED CUTOFF EXISTS — add to the class predicate that the constrained argmin T_*(P)=argmin_{tau: Q_P(tau)>=qbar} K_P(tau) is NONEMPTY (attained), e.g. K_P lower-semicontinuous on the compact feasible set, or T_*(P) nonempty stated directly. This is a standard regularity restriction on the law class, not a scope weakening. (2) hidden_rho_shrinking_use @ lem:oracle-snooping-coverage: STATE the decay condition explicitly in the lemma — add 'rho_n -> 0' (rho_n downarrow 0) as a hypothesis of lem:oracle-snooping-coverage so the deterministic cutoffs |tau-tau_*(P)|<=rho_n h_n eventually lie in the fixed window [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0]; wire it through depends_on / the lemma statement, and ensure conj:postselect-honest-ci carries rho_n -> 0 among its conditions. Do not leave it as a hidden in-proof assumption. (3) missing_kernel_regularity @ def:selected-ll-estimator: emit a proposed_definition_change (direction:correct) adding STANDARD KERNEL REGULARITY to the local-linear estimator's construction — K is a bounded, symmetric kernel with compact support (or sub-Gaussian tails), integral K = 1, and positive mass near 0 (nondegenerate second moment), so the weighted local-linear intercept and its scale hat sigma_n are well-defined (rules out K==0 / degenerate weights). Reference CalonicoCattaneoTitiunik2014RDRobustCI for the standard RD kernel conditions. After the def-corrections are applied, RE-DERIVE the affected nodes against the strengthened defs and confirm hat sigma_n, hat B_n(M), K are pinned at node level (resolves underspecified-rd-scale). These are the LAST regularity gaps; keep all earlier citation-lemma structure (lem:perfect-spline-extremal / generated-score-expansion / oracle-snooping-coverage) and derived glue intact.

changed.from: hat V_ll(M) is the intercept from the local-linear weighted least-squares regression of Y_{l,i} - Y_{s,i} - kappa on 1 and hat C_i - hat tau using kernel weights K((hat C_i - hat tau)/h_n) over observations with |hat C_i - hat tau| <= h_n
changed.to: hat V_ll(M) is the intercept from the local-linear weighted least-squares regression of Y_{l,i} - Y_{s,i} - kappa on 1 and hat C_i - hat tau using kernel weights K((hat C_i - hat tau)/h_n) over observations with |hat C_i - hat tau| <= h_n, where K is a bounded symmetric kernel with compact support, integral 1, positive mass on a neighborhood of 0, and nondegenerate second moment 0 < integral u^2 K(u) du < infinity
changed.reason: The oracle-scale and honest-CI lemmas need the local-linear fit, its Gram matrix, the standard-error term hat sigma_n, and the curvature-bias term hat B_n(M) to be well defined. As written, the definition allows degenerate choices such as K identically zero. Standard RD kernel regularity of the Calonico-Cattaneo-Titiunik type is therefore part of the estimator's construction, not an extra theorem-side restriction.

directive: D0.5 DEEP-SCRUTINY FIX (round 6 — honest narrow + de-launder + bookkeeping; address EVERY open finding). (1) def:generated-score-drift-handle [self_discharging_envelope] + lem:ghat-envelope-valid [dropped_gap_term]: emit a proposed_definition_change rewriting hat G_n from a single whole-gap envelope into EXPLICIT PRIMITIVE ADDITIVE COMPONENTS: hat G_n = hat g_gen + hat g_shift + hat g_rem, where hat g_gen bounds ONLY the generated-score term (controlled by lem:generated-score-expansion, second-order under r_n=o(h_n)), hat g_shift bounds ONLY the target-shift term (= B_slope(M,h_0)*rho_n h_n via lem:selected-local-slope-envelope + localization), and hat g_rem the stated second-order remainder. Then RE-DERIVE lem:ghat-envelope-valid: domination follows by adding the per-component bounds — NOT by assuming a whole-gap envelope (that was the self-discharge). Each component must trace to its source lemma, no dropped term. (2) lem:perfect-spline-active-set + conj:active-set-geometry [NONAFFINE_REDUCTION_GAP / bad_parametric_reduction]: the piecewise-AFFINE claim is NOT supported once knot/contact locations are free, M-dependent variables — do NOT claim affine. Emit a proposed_statement_change NARROWING both: keep only what is PROVED — (a) L_*(P,M) convex nonincreasing, U_*(P,M) concave nondecreasing and continuous (lem:endpoint-shape-in-budget); (b) FINITELY MANY smooth pieces / a finite family of candidate active patterns from lem:perfect-spline-extremal (finite knot/contact-pattern count); (c) M_*(P) well-defined with the sign-certification dichotomy from prop:critical-curvature. DROP 'affine on each cell'. State the residual exact piecewise-affine question as an OEQ (open). This is narrowing toward truth, not weakening-to-prove. (3) thm:selected-sharp-set [HEADLINE_REDUCES_TO_CLASS_IMAGE]: make the SUBSTANCE explicit so it is not a definitional image-unfold — the content is that the compatible-value set is a NONDEGENERATE INTERVAL [L_*,U_*] (convexity of H_*(P,M) + continuity of point-evaluation => image is an interval, every intermediate value attained), with L_*,U_* the extremal endpoints; phrase the theorem as the interval/attainment characterization, not '{mu(tau_*)} = the set'. If the endpoints lack an independent computable form, say the computable handle is the perfect-spline extremal family (lem:perfect-spline-extremal) + the finite-pattern bracket, not a closed form. (4) prop:critical-curvature [support_atoms_mismatch / BOOKKEEPING_FROM_PREVIOUS_INTERVAL]: fix the local atom-support consistency and interval bookkeeping (align the atom set / window references across the statement and its deps). (5) lem:oracle-snooping-coverage [missing_oracle_rd_premises]: add the missing standard RD premises to the citation-lemma's stated hypotheses (kernel regularity, local density bounded away from 0, variance continuity, bandwidth/effective-sample conditions) so the Armstrong-Kolesar oracle citation covers the claim in the generality used. All other items are local. Keep the citation-lemma structure (perfect-spline-extremal / generated-score-expansion / oracle-snooping-coverage) and derived glue. Goal: a coherent honest field-tier core where every claim is either proved, a cited classical node, or an explicitly-flagged OEQ.

changed.from: Suppose the window [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0] contains finitely many score atoms, 0 notin [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], and M_*(P) in (0,infinity). Then there exists a finite partition of [0,M_*(P)] such that on each partition cell both L_*(P,M) and U_*(P,M) are affine in M. Moreover zero first enters [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] at M=M_*(P), and M_*(P) is either (i) a partition boundary where the identity of an endpoint-attaining extremizer changes because a curvature or range inequality in H_*(P,M) switches binding status, or (ii) the unique zero of the active affine endpoint on a partition cell whose endpoint-attaining extremizer does not change across that cell.
changed.to: Suppose the window [tau_*(P)-h_0, tau_*(P)+h_0] contains finitely many score atoms, H_*(P,M) is nonempty for every M in [0,M_*(P)], 0 notin [L_*(P,0), U_*(P,0)], and M_*(P) in (0,infinity). Then there exists a finite family of candidate endpoint-attaining perfect-spline combinatorial types on [0,M_*(P)]. Consequently M -> L_*(P,M) is continuous, convex, and weakly decreasing on [0,M_*(P)], M -> U_*(P,M) is continuous, concave, and weakly increasing on [0,M_*(P)], and zero first enters [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] at M=M_*(P). The stronger claim that both endpoint maps are affine on a finite partition of [0,M_*(P)] is left open.
changed.reason: The perfect-spline reduction gives finitely many candidate active patterns, but the knot and range-contact locations remain free M-dependent variables. After that reduction the endpoint problem is still nonlinear in those locations, so the parametric-LP sensitivity argument does not justify affine dependence on M. What is provable from the frozen primitives is the monotonic/convex-concave continuity geometry together with the sign-certification frontier, not the stronger finite-partition affine claim.

changed.from: Construct hat G_n as a data-only envelope for the entire selected-score feasibility gap: combine (a) an orthogonalized, cross-fitted first-order correction for replacing the population score C with the generated score hat C in the local-linear score, with (b) a selected-threshold localization envelope for the additional target drift created by evaluating the local-linear fit at the random selected cutoff hat tau instead of the population cutoff tau_*(P), and then add a remainder envelope for the residual second-order generated-score and score-discretization terms.
changed.to: Construct hat G_n additively as hat G_n = hat g_{gen,n} + hat g_{shift,n} + hat g_{rem,n}, where hat g_{gen,n} is a data-only envelope for the orthogonalized first-order generated-score perturbation created by replacing the population score C with the generated score hat C in the local-linear score, hat g_{shift,n} is a data-only envelope for the selected-threshold target shift |g_P(hat tau)-g_P(tau_*(P))| induced by evaluating at the random selected cutoff hat tau rather than the population cutoff tau_*(P), and hat g_{rem,n} is a data-only envelope for the residual second-order generated-score and score-discretization remainder.
changed.reason: The proof of the feasible-to-oracle comparison must bound three distinct primitive terms separately: the first-order generated-score perturbation, the first-order target-shift term from \(\hat\tau-\tau_*(P)\), and the residual second-order remainder. Treating \(\hat G_n\) as a single undifferentiated whole-gap envelope is self-discharging and obscures which lemma controls which term. The additive decomposition is the correct formula because it lets `lem:ghat-envelope-valid` derive the overall bound by summing the componentwise envelopes.
